title: tzeltalia amphitricha
intro: Tzeltalia amphitricha, also known as Yucatec sedge, is a type of plant species in the Carex family that is native to the Yucatan Peninsula in Mexico. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Tzeltalia amphitricha include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to reduce swelling and prevent infection. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and poultices, are often used to treat wounds and skin irritations, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine.
